title: Learning from Home
passage:
Learning from Home

Online learning is very popular today. Many students do not go to a traditional school building. Instead, they study at home using a computer and the internet. This is also called distance education.

Meet Sarah. She is a twelve-year-old student from Canada. Sarah does not go to a normal school because her house is very far from the city. Every morning at 9:00 AM, Sarah opens her laptop. She logs into her online classroom. She can see her teacher, Mr. Brown, on the screen. She can also see her classmates. They live in different parts of Canada.

Online classes are very interesting. Mr. Brown teaches math, science, and English. He uses colorful slides and videos. Sarah can type her questions in a chat box, or she can speak using a microphone. Her classes finish at 1:00 PM. In the afternoon, Sarah does her homework on her computer and sends it to her teacher by email.

There are many good things about online learning. First, students do not need to travel to school. This saves a lot of time. Second, students can learn at their own speed. If they do not understand a lesson, they can watch the video of the class again. They can also study in comfortable clothes.

However, online learning also has some problems. Sometimes, the internet connection is bad, and the video stops. Also, some students feel lonely because they cannot play with their friends during break time. Sarah misses her friends, but she likes online learning because she can sleep longer in the morning.

In conclusion, online learning is a great way to study for many children and adults around the world. It is changing how people learn.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why does Sarah not go to a normal school?

options.B: Her house is far from the city.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Sarah does not go to a normal school because her house is very far from the city.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What time does Sarah's online class start?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Every morning at 9:00 AM, Sarah opens her laptop. She logs into her online classroom.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How does Sarah send her homework to Mr. Brown?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Sarah does her homework on her computer and sends it to her teacher by email.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do some students feel lonely during online learning?

options.C: They do not like their teacher.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'Also, some students feel lonely because they cannot play with their friends during break time.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'She is a twelve-year-old student from Canada.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Sarah's teacher is named Mr. Brown.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 mentions: 'She can see her teacher, Mr. Brown, on the screen.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Sarah's classmates all live in the same city as her.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They live in different parts of Canada.' Therefore, they do not live in the same city as her.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Mr. Brown teaches math, science, and English, but it does not mention Sarah's favorite subject.

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What does Sarah use to open her online classroom?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Every morning at 9:00 AM, Sarah opens her laptop. She logs into her online classroom.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What can students watch again if they do not understand a lesson?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'If they do not understand a lesson, they can watch the video of the class again.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What can Sarah do longer in the morning?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'she likes online learning because she can sleep longer in the morning.'

vocabulary.meaning: following ideas or methods that have existed for a long time
a system of education in which people study at home with the internet
people who are in the same class at school
unhappy because you are not with other people

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Dạng bài Multiple Choice: Đọc kỹ câu hỏi để xác định từ khóa chính (ví dụ: 'Why', 'What time'). Quét nhanh trong bài đọc để tìm đoạn chứa từ khóa đó. Vì đây là trình độ A1-A2, đáp án đúng sẽ dùng từ ngữ giống hệt hoặc cực kỳ gần gũi với bài đọc.

2. Dạng bài True/False/Not Given: Đối chiếu trực tiếp từng câu khẳng định với bài đọc. Nếu thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn (ví dụ: 'same city' đối lập với 'different parts'), chọn FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc đến (ví dụ: môn học yêu thích), chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Dạng bài Short-answer Questions: Chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Tìm chính xác từ vựng xuất hiện trong văn bản để điền vào câu trả lời, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ.
